title: gh#119: Tests: Extract and cover MediaDropZone file validation
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/119.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Problem

`packages/web/src/components/posts/MediaDropZone.tsx` contains a meaningful `validateFiles(...)` helper, but it is private to the component and coupled directly to `toast.error(...)`. That makes the platform media-limit rules harder to test directly and encourages future changes to rely only on component-level behavior.

This was originally listed inside #28, but it is a web/component concern and should not be bundled with shared storage backend tests.

## Decision

Extract the validation logic into a pure, testable helper that returns valid files plus validation errors. Keep toast rendering inside the component.

Suggested shape:

```ts
export function validateMediaFiles(input): {
  validFiles: File[];
  errors: string[];
}
```

`MediaDropZone` should call the helper, toast each returned error, and pass only valid files to `onFilesSelected`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `MediaDropZone` no longer owns unexported toast-coupled validation logic.
- Unit tests cover representative validation cases:
  - no selected platform
  - unsupported MIME type
  - image/video file size limits
  - image/video mixing rejection
  - max image count and max video count enforcement
  - valid files are returned unchanged
- Existing `MediaDropZone` user interactions still work.
- `pnpm --filter @sms/web test -- MediaDropZone` passes, or the closest focused web test command if the test file name differs.

## Out of scope

- Changing platform media limits.
- Changing upload transport or API behavior.
- Redesigning the drop zone UI.

## References

- Split out of #28 during issue grooming.

recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#119 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
